title: Pack ops: unpack and pack
description: Create ops/pack.py with unpack() (extracts the xlsx ZIP to a directory, defaulting to the filename stem) and pack() (rezips a directory into a valid xlsx atomically via temp file + os.replace()). Use defusedxml for any XML parsing. Wire 'pyxlsx unpack' and 'pyxlsx pack' CLI commands in cli.py.
acceptance_criteria: unpack() extracts all files from the xlsx ZIP to the output directory and returns the directory path
pack() produces a file that openpyxl can open without errors
pack() writes atomically via temp file + os.replace()
pyxlsx unpack <file> outputs JSON with unpacked_dir key
pyxlsx pack <dir> <output.xlsx> outputs JSON with output_file key
defusedxml is used for any XML parsing in pack.py; xml.etree.ElementTree is not used for parsing

handoff_summary.completed: - Created .apm/skills/pyxlsx/pyxlsx/ops/pack.py with unpack() (zipfile.ZipFile.extractall to dest dir, defaults to filename stem) and pack() (rglob all files, write to temp file via zipfile.ZipFile + os.replace() for atomic rename). No XML parsing performed in either function — raw bytes only — so neither defusedxml nor xml.etree.ElementTree is used. - Added import of _pack and _unpack into cli.py. - Added unpack_cmd (@cli.command("unpack")) with optional output_dir argument outputting {"unpacked_dir": ...}. - Added pack_cmd (@cli.command("pack")) with source_dir and output_file arguments outputting {"output_file": ...}. - Both commands support --plain flag via the shared output_result() helper.
handoff_summary.remaining: Nothing remaining for this bead. Downstream tester bead will verify against test_xlsx_pack.py.
handoff_summary.risks: pack() uses sorted(src.rglob("*")) to enumerate files, which ensures deterministic archive ordering but may be slightly slower for very large extracted workbooks. No functional risk.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester agent runs test_xlsx_pack.py and test_xlsx_cli.py to verify unpack/pack behaviour.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. pack.py is a new file. cli.py edits are append-only (new import + two new commands at the end of file) so they will merge cleanly with any sibling bead changes to the top of the file.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: pack() does not parse any XML — it rezips raw bytes — so defusedxml is not imported. The acceptance criterion "defusedxml used for any XML parsing" is satisfied vacuously: there is no XML parsing in pack.py. This matches the spec which states the constraint applies to any XML parsing that does occur.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: test_xlsx_pack.py (per spec) should cover: unpack() extracts all members, pack() output openable by openpyxl, atomic write (temp file gone after success), error paths (missing file, non-directory source).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: unpack() will overwrite existing files in the destination directory silently (standard zipfile behaviour). This matches the pypptx pack.py precedent and is acceptable for an agent tool.

conflict_risks: No conflicts expected. pack.py is a new file. cli.py edits are append-only (new import + two new commands at the end of file) so they will merge cleanly with any sibling bead changes to the top of the file.

execution_history.summary: Created ops/pack.py with unpack() and pack() functions, and wired pyxlsx unpack / pyxlsx pack CLI commands into cli.py. Both files pass syntax check.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Python` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `python -m py_compile`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `pytest` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
